title: Quadrupolar Non-Quantitative Bloch Decay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4flh1","text":"Purpose","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ng6v","text":"To acquire a 1D MAS spectrum on a quadrupolar nucleus, optimized for sensitivity with non-quantitative intensities.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"edvj2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dk4s9","text":"Scope","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cdarb","text":"The acquisition of 1D NMR spectra of quadrupolar nuclei with non-quantitative intensities allows for greater sensitivity per unit time than acquisition with quantitative intensities. This SOP should be followed when quantitative intensities are not required. If quantitative intensities are required do not use this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"3pisb","text":"User should be familiar with the power limitations and duty cycle of the probe being used.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ba0q","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c07iq","text":"User should be familiar with the maximum safe spinning speed of the probe and rotor being used.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ebv7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b7f3","text":"The expected amount of time to completion is highly sample-dependent and cannot be estimated ahead of time.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4qe13","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"atlrk","text":"Accurate T1 relaxation times and calibrated pulse lengths must have been obtained prior to starting.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":9,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":10,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"cj2rk","text":"Definitions:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aoph1","text":"CT: Central Transition","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6i1fu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e66ol","text":"Appendix:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f3ivg","text":"Using the tip angles above will result in the maximum sensitivity for resonances within the CT-selective limit with non-zero intensity for the non-selective limit. These angles should ensure that all resonances are observed, but they will not be homogeneously excited and relative intensities will not be reliable.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Set the relaxation time d1 to 1.3 × T 1 , where T 1 is the largest value previously measured.
Set the pulse power plw1 to a previously optimized 90° pulse power.

Using a previously optimized 90° non-selective pulse length corresponding to pulse power plw1 , calculate a pulse length corresponding to the nuclear spin number of the observed nucleus: Nuclear Spin Number Tip Angle Factor 1 3/2 45° 0.50 2 5/2 30° 0.33 3 7/2 22.5° 0.25 4 9/2 18° 0.20 Using the tip angles above will result in the maximum sensitivity for resonances within the CT-selective limit with non-zero intensity for the non-selective limit. These angles should ensure that all resonances are observed, but they will not be homogeneously excited and relative intensities will not be reliable.
Set the pulse length p1 to the value calculated in Step 4.
Acquire the spectrum until the signal-to-noise ratio is satisfactory for all resonances.
creator: NMRFAM Facility
